'''Wiorkettle''' was a [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvian]] on [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] during [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian's]] [[Baron Administrator/Legends|administration]]. He worked for [[Planet Dreams, Incorporated]], a company that [[Terraforming/Legends|terraformed]] [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] and inhospitable planets and used them to create habitable [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmospheres]].<ref name="GG3" />

==Biography==
Wiorkettle was Planet Dreams' chief artist, responsible for the aesthetics of the environments they created. He was an equal partner in the company along with the [[Lutrillian/Legends|Lutrillian]] [[Treva Horme]] and [[Ozz (Ugnaught)|Ozz]] the [[Ugnaught/Legends|Ugnaught]]. In approximately [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], the partners relocated their head offices to [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin's]] [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]].<ref name="GG3" />

The three Planet Dreams executives were considering selling private hovering platforms in Bespin's [[Life Zone/Legends|Life Zone]] when Ozz revealed that someone was embezzling money and terraforming equipment from the company. Ozz vowed to investigate but was interrupted in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] when the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] arrived and [[Occupation of Bespin|took control]] of the city. The partners decided to download all of their files and evacuate, but while Horme and Wiorkettle followed the plan, Ozz disappeared. The remaining partners moved back to the [[Oseon asteroid belt]] after fleeing Bespin.<ref name="GG3" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Treva_Horme_and_Wiorkettle_TESB.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Treva Horme and Wiorkettle on Cloud City]]
Wiorkettle was a background character played by actor [[Cathy Munro]]<ref name="SW Interviews">{{WebCite|url=https://starwarsinterviews.com/original-trilogy/episode-v-the-empire-strikes-back/cathy-munroe-zuckuss|text=Cathy Munroe interview|work=Star Wars Interviews|archivedate=20210227184925|nolive=1}}</ref> in {{Film|V}}, but it is unclear if he can be seen in the final film. The character was most prominently seen in behind the scenes images and promotional photos for the film, in every case paired with another background character, [[Treva Horme]]. Artwork from a [[1980]] [[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back Coloring Book (Cloud City)|coloring book]] based on the film is patterned after one of these promotional photos.<ref name="Coloring">[[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back Coloring Book (Cloud City)|''Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back Coloring Book'' (Cloud City)]]</ref>

Wiorkettle and Horme did not received names until the second edition of [[West End Games]]' [[1996]] [[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back'']].<ref name="GG3" /> The names were officially applied to the film characters in the ''[[Cloud City Limited]]'' set of the ''[[Star Wars Customizable Card Game]]'' in [[1997]].<ref name="CCG" />
